$comment_1: Halvren Checklist — canonical 10-question source of truth.
$comment_2: Used by: scripts/build_operators.py (operator scorecard), api/checklist/score.js (live scoring tool), checklist.html (landing — duplicated by hand for now).
$comment_3: When updating: update this file first, regenerate operator pages with python3 scripts/build_operators.py, then update /checklist.html and /index.html to match.

questions.question_html: Does it generate free cash flow through the full cycle, or only the top half of it?
questions.default_note: Reported earnings lie in commodities. Ten years of FCF tells the truth.

questions.question_html: Do the unit economics still work at the worst price of the last decade?
questions.default_note: If the thesis needs a new commodity regime to work, it isn't a business yet.

questions.question_html: What does the balance sheet look like at trough pricing: net debt, covenants, maturity ladder?
questions.default_note: The next crisis won't ask what you projected. It asks what you owe and when.

questions.question_html: When they reinvest a dollar (capex, M&A, or buyback), what actually comes back?
questions.default_note: ROIC on incremental capital, not reported ROE on the whole book.

questions.question_html: How much of the operator's own net worth, bought and not granted, sits in this name?
questions.default_note: Options are loyalty to a quarter. Open-market purchases are loyalty to a decade.

questions.question_html: What did management actually do in 2015 and 2020: issue, buy back, or sit still?
questions.default_note: Every operator on the desk has been stress-tested twice in the last decade. The record is public.

questions.question_html: Is compensation tied to per-share value, or to production, revenue, and size?
questions.default_note: Growth at the expense of the share count is a tax the operator quietly charges you.

questions.question_html: Who succeeds the operator, and is that person already visible on the page?
questions.default_note: Every owner-operator story ends. The question is whether the business does too.

questions.question_html: Where are we on the cost curve that matters: the real one, not the one in the pitch deck?
questions.default_note: Every operator is first-quartile when they pick the quartile. Ask who sets the denominator.

questions.question_html: What does a “normal” year look like a decade from now, and does this business still work at that price?
questions.default_note: If the thesis only works at the top of the cycle, it's a trade. Halvren doesn't trade.
